meta.name: Systems_Architecture_MAX
meta.description: MAX-density systems architecture kernel integrating distributed systems, microservices, and deterministic infrastructure.

kernel.scope: Cross-domain technology architecture integrating distributed systems, reliability engineering, and deterministic computing.

rules: DIST_RULE_1: CAP theorem: choose 2 of Consistency, Availability, Partition-tolerance.
DIST_RULE_2: Eventual consistency acceptable for many distributed use cases.
DIST_RULE_3: Fault tolerance requires redundancy and graceful degradation.
DIST_RULE_4: Horizontal scaling preferred over vertical scaling.
REL_RULE_1: Circuit breakers prevent cascade failures.
REL_RULE_2: Exponential backoff with jitter for retries.
REL_RULE_3: Full observability: metrics, logs, traces.
REL_RULE_4: Chaos engineering validates failure handling.
SEC_RULE_1: Defense in depth with multiple security layers.
SEC_RULE_2: Principle of least privilege for all access.
SEC_RULE_3: Zero trust: verify every request regardless of origin.
SEC_RULE_4: Complete audit logging for all security events.

invariants: INV_TECH_1: All state changes must be auditable and reversible.
INV_TECH_2: System must degrade gracefully under load.
INV_TECH_3: No single point of failure in critical paths.
references: Brewer, E. (2000). Towards Robust Distributed Systems.
Nygard, M.T. (2007). Release It! Design and Deploy Production-Ready Software.
